study_title: An electromyographic and kinetic comparison of conventional and Romanian deadlifts
journal: Journal of Exercise Science & Fitness
outcome_measures: Direct and/or indirect measures relevant to the stated topic
main_result: In 21 males, conventional and Romanian deadlifts differed in lower-limb muscle activity and joint kinetics; conventional deadlift showed greater rectus femoris and gluteus maximus demand.
statistical_significance: See source; not reducible to one test
practical_significance: In 21 males, conventional and Romanian deadlifts differed in lower-limb muscle activity and joint kinetics; conventional deadlift showed greater rectus femoris and gluteus maximus demand.
study_limitations: Heterogeneous protocols and populations; male-specific aggregate often unavailable
conflicting_evidence: See evidence conclusions and gaps tables
reviewer_notes: Supports variation-specific mechanics; acute EMG and kinetics do not prove longitudinal hypertrophy magnitude.
